palette_hint: Background #1a1714 (near-black warm charcoal), body text #e8e0d4 (warm ivory), accent gold #c9a84c and deep burgundy #7a2d3b.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif at weights 400 and 700 with wide letter-spacing; body in system-ui at weight 300 for lightness; pull quotes in italic Georgia 400 at 1.4em; overall feel is understated Parisian luxury.
description: Maison Cendrée is a fictional Lyonnaise-inspired bistro in Montreal whose static site uses a deliberate typographic hierarchy—drop caps, pull quotes, and a modular scale from 0.75rem to 4rem—to evoke the atmosphere of candlelit stone dining rooms.
constraints: All pages share a centered content column max-width 860px with horizontal padding 2.5rem; the charcoal #1a1714 background bleeds full-width on every page.
Typographic scale must use exactly four sizes: 0.8rem (captions/labels), 1rem (body), 1.65rem (section headings), and 3.8rem (page-title display text), all in rem units.
Heading letter-spacing: display titles at 0.18em, section headings at 0.12em, body copy at 0.01em; line-height 1.15 for headings and 1.75 for body paragraphs.

Every page that contains a long-form text section (our-story, menu descriptions) must use a ::first-letter drop cap styled at 3.4em Georgia, float left, line-height 0.85, color #c9a84c, margin-right 0.15em.
Pull quotes appear on at least two pages, set in italic Georgia, font-size 1.65rem, color #c9a84c, border-left 3px solid #7a2d3b, padding-left 1.4rem, max-width 580px, centered within the column.

page_specs.menu: Four seasonal sections—Amuse-Bouche, Entrées, Plats, Desserts—each introduced with a 1.65rem section heading and a two-sentence description paragraph that opens with a drop cap. Every dish is listed as a two-column row: dish name in Georgia 700 small-caps on the left and price in #c9a84c on the right, separated by a 1px dotted border-bottom in #3a3530. A pull quote midway through the page reads: 'We butcher whole animals on Tuesdays; the menu is never quite the same twice.'
page_specs.cellar: A curated wine and spirits page organized into three vertical sections: Vins Naturels, Bourgognes, and Digestifs, each with a 1.65rem heading and a short italic Georgia paragraph describing the philosophy of that category. Bottle listings follow the same two-column name/price pattern as the menu. One pull quote in burgundy-bordered gold italic: 'Our cellar holds fewer than 200 labels—each chosen over a long dinner, never a tasting room.'
page_specs.reservations: A single centered text block explains the reservation policy in two body paragraphs (walk-ins welcome Tuesday–Thursday; reservations required Friday–Sunday, parties of 2–8 only) followed by a mailto anchor styled as a full-width ivory bordered button at 1rem letter-spacing 0.14em. Below that, a pull quote: 'Tables turn once per evening. You are never rushed.' Contact phone number and hours printed in 0.8rem label style beneath.
page_specs.our-story: A long-form narrative page about chef Élise Fournier's apprenticeship in Lyon and her move to Montreal's Plateau-Mont-Royal neighbourhood in 2011. Opens with a 3.8rem display title 'Une Table, Deux Villes' and a 340-word story split into three paragraphs, the first using a drop cap. A pull quote mid-page reads: 'The bouchon taught me that a great meal is an act of stubbornness against convenience.' Closes with a small italic caption crediting photographer Noémie Archambault.
page_specs.find-us: Address, neighbourhood context, and transit directions for 4221 rue Saint-Denis, Plateau-Mont-Royal, Montréal. Opens with a 3.8rem display title and a short paragraph describing the unmarked black door between a florist and a bookshop. Hours table rendered in a two-column layout (day range left, hours right) using 0.8rem label sizing and #c9a84c for the time values. Ends with a note in 0.8rem italic: 'Street parking available after 19 h; Laurier métro 6 minutes on foot.'
